questions.text: The direction of the magnetic lines of force can be found by using:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The right-hand rule helps determine the direction of the magnetic field around a current-carrying wire. By pointing your thumb in the direction of the current, your curled fingers indicate the circular direction of the magnetic field lines.
Henry's law relates to gas solubility in liquids and is unrelated to electromagnetism or the direction of magnetic fields.
The left-hand rule is used for determining the direction of force on a conductor in a magnetic field, not the direction of magnetic field lines due to current.
Faraday's law explains electromagnetic induction and how a changing magnetic field can induce an electric current. It does not describe the direction of magnetic field lines.

questions.explanation: The right-hand rule is specifically designed to find the direction of the magnetic field created by a current-carrying wire. By pointing your thumb in the direction of the current flow, your fingers naturally curl in the direction of the magnetic field lines. This is different from Henry's law, which pertains to gas solubility, and Faraday's law, which discusses electromagnetic induction. Similarly, the left-hand rule is used to determine the direction of force in a magnetic field, not the magnetic field itself.

questions.text: Magnetic field will not produce in case of?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because A positive charge, top, moving perpendicularly through a magnetic field is deflected. The right-hand rule, bottom, predicts the direction in which the positive charge will be deflected. A similar left-hand rule predicts the deflection of negative charges.

This option is incorrect because A negative charge introduces a negative sign, which flips the direction of the force. So, for a negative charge your right hand lies to you, and the force on the negative charge will be opposite to the direction indicated by your right hand.

This option is correct because Magnetic fields will not be produced in case of neutral particles. The protons, cathode rays and alpha particles are charged particles, so the magnetic field deflects them. But there is no charge on the neutrons, so they are not deflected by the magnetic field.
This option is incorrect because Option C is correct.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C. Magnetic fields will not be produced in case of neutral particles. The protons, cathode rays and alpha particles are charged particles, so the magnetic field deflects them. But there is no charge on the neutrons, so they are not deflected by the magnetic field.

questions.text: How can a magnetic field be produced?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A permanent magnet has a persistent magnetic field due to the alignment of magnetic domains within the material.
An electric current flowing through a conductor generates a magnetic field around it, as described by Ampère's law.
A temporary magnet, such as an electromagnet, can produce a magnetic field when an electric current is applied, but not when the current is off.

options.text: Using a permanent magnet or electric current
options.explanation: Both permanent magnets and electric currents can produce magnetic fields. Permanent magnets have a constant field, whereas electric currents produce a field when flowing through a conductor.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Using a permanent magnet or electric current. Permanent magnets inherently have a magnetic field due to the alignment of magnetic domains, while electric currents generate a magnetic field around the conductor through which they flow. This is consistent with the principles of electromagnetism. Option A is incorrect because while permanent magnets do produce magnetic fields, this option does not account for the role of electric currents. Option B is incorrect as it only considers electric currents and not permanent magnets. Option C is misleading as temporary magnets do produce magnetic fields, but they require an electric current to do so, which is not mentioned in the option.

questions.text: Electric charge in uniform motion produces:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An electric field is a region of space around an electric charge where other charges experience a force. The strength of the electric field is proportional to the magnitude of the charge and inversely proportional to the square of the distance from the charge.

A magnetic field is a region of space around a magnet or a moving electric charge where other magnets or moving electric charges experience a force. The strength of the magnetic field is proportional to the strength of the magnet or the current in the wire and inversely proportional to the square of the distance from the magnet or wire.

When an electric charge is in uniform motion, it produces both an electric field and a magnetic field. This is known as electromagnetic radiation.Electric field: An electric field is a region of space around an electric charge where other charges experience a force. The strength of the electric field is proportional to the magnitude of the charge and inversely proportional to the square of the distance from the charge.Magnetic field: A magnetic field is a region of space around a magnet or a moving electric charge where other magnets or moving electric charges experience a force. The strength of the magnetic field is proportional to the strength of the magnet or the current in the wire and inversely proportional to the square of the distance from the magnet or wire.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: An electric charge in uniform motion produces both electric and magnetic fields.A stationary electric charge produces only an electric field. However, a moving electric charge produces both an electric field and a magnetic field. The electric field is produced by the charge's static electric field, while the magnetic field is produced by the charge's moving electric field.The strength of the electric field produced by a moving charge is proportional to the charge's magnitude and its speed. The direction of the electric field is perpendicular to the direction of the charge's motion.The strength of the magnetic field produced by a moving charge is proportional to the charge's magnitude, its speed, and its perpendicular distance from the field point. The direction of the magnetic field is perpendicular to both the direction of the charge's motion and the direction from the charge to the field point.The following are some examples of electric charges in uniform motion that produce both electric and magnetic fields:A charged particle moving through a vacuumA current-carrying wireA rotating magnet

questions.text: When currents are moving in the same direction in two conductors, the force will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The force between two conductors with currents flowing in the same direction is attraction.This is because the magnetic fields produced by the two currents are aligned in the same direction. When two magnetic fields are aligned in the same direction, they attract each other.The force of attraction between two conductors with currents flowing in the same direction is given by the following equation:F = μ0I1I2 / 2πrwhere:F is the force of attractionμ0 is the permeability of free spaceI1 and I2 are the currents in the two conductorsr is the distance between the conductors

Repulsive: This is the force that would occur if the currents were flowing in opposite directions. The magnetic fields produced by the two currents would be aligned in opposite directions, and when two magnetic fields are aligned in opposite directions, they repel each other.

Retracting: This is a type of repulsive force that would occur if the currents were flowing in opposite directions and the conductors were close together. The magnetic fields produced by the two currents would create a force that would tend to pull the conductors apart.

Opposing: This is a type of repulsive force that would occur if the currents were flowing in opposite directions and the conductors were far apart. The magnetic fields produced by the two currents would create a force that would tend to slow down the currents.

questions.explanation: The force between two conductors with currents flowing in the same direction is attraction. This is because the magnetic fields produced by the two currents are aligned in the same direction. When two magnetic fields are aligned in the same direction, they attract each other.The force of attraction between two conductors with currents flowing in the same direction is given by the following equation:F = μ0I1I2 / 2πrwhere:F is the force of attractionμ0 is the permeability of free spaceI1 and I2 are the currents in the two conductorsr is the distance between the conductors

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to Formula of Magnetic Force between two wires,F= I1I2L2dF I1 I2 l F 12dF is directly proportional to Permeability, Current of two wires and length.F is inversely proportional to 2 and distance.

According to Formula of Magnetic Force between two wires,F= I1I2L2dF I1 I2 l F 12dF is directly proportional to Permeability, Current of two wires and length.F is inversely proportional to 2 and distance.

According to Formula of Magnetic Force between two wires,F= I1I2L2dF I1 I2 l F 12dF is directly proportional to Permeability, Current of two wires and length.F is inversely proportional to 2 and distance.
This is incorrect

questions.explanation: According to Formula of Magnetic Force between two wires,F= I1I2L2dF I1 I2 l F 12dF is directly proportional to Permeability, Current of two wires and length.F is inversely proportional to 2 and distance.

questions.text: Two long, parallel conductors which are free to move are arranged 1.0 cm apart. A steady current of 20 A flows in each of the conductors in the same direction. The conductors:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Remains stationary: This would only be true if there was no current or if the currents were flowing in opposite directions.
When two parallel wires carry current in the same direction, they exert equal and opposite attractive forces on each other. This causes them to move towards one another.
Move away from each other: This would happen if the currents were flowing in opposite directions, creating a repulsive force.

options.text: Move at right angles to each other
options.explanation: Move at right angles to each other: The force between the conductors is either attractive or repulsive, acting along the line connecting them, not at right angles.

questions.explanation: When two parallel wires carry current in the same direction, they exert equal and opposite attractive forces on each other. This causes them to move towards one another.

questions.text: A long straight current-carrying conductor has current directed from bottom to top when held vertically. What will be the direction of magnetic field lines when observed from below the conductor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. According to the right-hand rule, if you position your thumb in the direction of the current (upwards), your fingers will curl in a clockwise direction when viewed from below.
This option is incorrect. If the current flows from bottom to top, the magnetic field lines do not curl in an anti-clockwise direction when viewed from below.
This option is incorrect. The magnetic field lines do not travel vertically upward; instead, they circulate around the wire.
This option is incorrect. The magnetic field lines do not move directly downward; they are oriented in a circular pattern around the wire.

questions.explanation: The magnetic field generated by a long straight current-carrying conductor can be determined using the right-hand rule. When the current flows from bottom to top, if you point your right thumb in the direction of the current, your fingers will curl around the wire. When viewed from below, the fingers will curl in a clockwise direction, indicating that the magnetic field lines are oriented clockwise. The other options are incorrect because they do not represent the actual behavior of magnetic field lines around a straight current-carrying wire.
hints: current directed from bottom to top

questions.text: Two parallel straight conductors, in which current is flowing in the same direction, attract each other. The cause of it is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them. This happens because:Magnetic Fields Around the Conductors:Each current-carrying conductor generates a circular magnetic field around it, as described by Ampère's right-hand rule.Magnetic Interaction:The magnetic field of one conductor exerts a force on the second conductor and vice versa.When currents are in the same direction, the magnetic fields between the wires reinforce each other in a way that causes an attractive force.Lorentz Force Explanation:The moving charges (current) in one conductor experience a force due to the magnetic field created by the other conductor.According to the Lorentz force law, this force acts inwards, pulling the conductors together.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.

questions.explanation: Correct option is 'A'.

questions.text: A unit of induction is said to exist at a point where the force per unit charge experienced by a positive test charge, moving with a velocity of 1ms-1 in the direction perpendicular to the field is 1 Newton. What is this phenomenon called?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A gravitational field is a region of space around a mass where another mass experiences a force of attraction. It is unrelated to the force experienced by a charge moving perpendicularly to a magnetic field.

The magnetic field is a region around a magnetic material or a moving electric charge within which the force of magnetism acts. A magnetic field is a force field that is created by moving electric charges and magnetic dipoles and exerts a force on other nearby moving charges and magnetic dipoles.
Magnetic flux is the measure of the number of magnetic field lines passing through a surface, not directly related to the force experienced by a moving charge.
Flux generally refers to the rate of flow of a property per unit area, and is not specific to the magnetic force experienced by a moving charge.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Magnetic Field. A magnetic field is defined by the force it exerts on moving charges. In this question, the force per unit charge experienced by a test charge moving perpendicularly to the field indicates the presence of a magnetic field. The force is proportional to the velocity of the charge and the strength of the magnetic field.Option A (Gravitational Field) is incorrect because it deals with forces due to gravity, not magnetic forces.Option C (Magnetic Flux) is incorrect because it describes the number of magnetic field lines passing through a surface, not the force on a moving charge.Option D (Flux) is too general and does not specifically relate to magnetic fields affecting moving charges.

questions.text: The direction of the magnetic line of force depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Nature of the material of the conducting wire
options.explanation: The direction of the magnetic lines of force does not depend upon the nature of the material of the conduction wire.
The direction of the magnetic lines of force does not depend upon the Area of the conducting wire.
The direction of the magnetic lines of force does not depend upon the Amount of the current.
The direction of the magnetic force depends on the direction of the current and the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.explanation: The direction of the magnetic force depends on the direction of the current and the direction of the magnetic field. Of these two, the direction of the current is in options so that will be the correct answer.

questions.text: A wire carries a current out of the page. Which diagram below correctly describes the magnetic field of the wire?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This figure represents the electric field of a positive charge.
Figure B represents the electric field of a negative charge.
This shows the direction of the magnetic field around a wire carrying current directed into the page.
This represents the magnetic field around a current carrying wire with current being directed out of the page.
Magnetic field between flat, opposite poles of magnets can be represented this way.

questions.explanation: The magnetic field lines form concentric circles around the wire. If you wrap your right hand’s fingers around the wire with your thumb pointing in the direction of the current, then the direction in which the fingers curl will give the direction of the magnetic field. As the current is directed out of the page, then the magnetic field will have a counter clockwise direction. So “D” is the correct answer.

questions.text: A current carrying conductor carries current away from you the direction of magnetic field with respect to you is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A current carrying conductor carries current away from you the direction of magnetic field with respect to you is clockwise by using right hand rule.
A current carrying conductor carries current away from you the direction of magnetic field with respect to you is clockwise by using right hand rule.
A current carrying conductor carries current away from you the direction of magnetic field with respect to you is clockwise by using right hand rule.
True if the direction of thecurrent is reversed.

questions.explanation: A current carrying conductor carries current away from you the direction of magnetic field with respect to you is clockwise by using right hand rule.

questions.text: Two parallel long wires carry the same current and repel each other with a force F per unit length. If both these currents are doubled and the wire separation tripled, the force per unit length becomes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per calculations.
Incorrect as per calculations.
Incorrect as per calculations.

When the two parallel long wires carry the same current and repel each other with a force F per unit length we have:F₁ / ℓ = I² B μ₀ / (2 π h)And when the currents are doubled and the wire separation tripled, the force per unit length becomes:F₂ / ℓ = (2I)² B μ₀ / (2 π (3h))F₂ / ℓ = 4 I² B μ₀ / (2 π (3h))F₂ / ℓ = (4/3) I² B μ₀ / (2 π h) = (4/3) F₁ / ℓTherefore, when the currents are doubled and the wire separation tripled, the force per unit length becomes 4/3 of the initial force per unit length

questions.explanation: When the two parallel long wires carry the same current and repel each other with a force F per unit length we have:F₁ / ℓ = I² B μ₀ / (2 π h)And when the currents are doubled and the wire separation tripled, the force per unit length becomes:F₂ / ℓ = (2I)² B μ₀ / (2 π (3h))F₂ / ℓ = 4 I² B μ₀ / (2 π (3h))F₂ / ℓ = (4/3) I² B μ₀ / (2 π h) = (4/3) F₁ / ℓTherefore, when the currents are doubled and the wire separation tripled, the force per unit length becomes 4/3 of the initial force per unit length.

questions.text: A transformer is used to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Convert alternating current to direct current
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as conversion of A.C to D.C is done by rectifier not transformer.

options.text: Convert direct current to alternating current
options.explanation: Option B is incorrect as the conversion of D.C to A.C is done by inverter not transformer

options.text: Convert mechanical energy to electrical energy
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect as the conversion of mechanical to electrical energy is done by generator not transformer

options.text: Change the level of alternating voltage
options.explanation: Option D is correct as it states the function of transformer.

questions.explanation: Option D is corect because transformer only works with alternating voltage and it is used increase or decrease the alternating voltages.

questions.text: Voltage in secondary winding is _ to the current in the secondary coil.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Voltage in the secondary coil is inversely proportional to the current in the secondary coil when the resistance is constant.
Voltage in the secondary coil is inversely proportional to the current in the secondary coil when the resistance is constant.
Voltage in the secondary coil is inversely proportional to the current in the secondary coil when the resistance is constant.
Voltage in the secondary coil is inversely proportional to the current in the secondary coil when the resistance is constant.

questions.explanation: Voltage in the secondary coil is inversely proportional to the current in the secondary coil when the resistance is constant.

questions.text: A real transformer does not change the _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A transformer is specifically designed to change the voltage level, either stepping it up or stepping it down, depending on the application. This is one of the primary functions of a transformer.

While transformers do affect the current level, the relationship between input and output current is determined by the turns ratio of the transformer. In practice, transformers adjust current levels to maintain power balance, except for unavoidable losses.

Real transformers experience some power loss due to inefficiencies, such as heat and magnetic losses. While an ideal transformer would keep power levels constant, real transformers experience slight reductions in power from input to output.
A real transformer does not alter the frequency of the electrical signal. The frequency remains constant from the input to the output, which is a fundamental characteristic of transformers.

questions.explanation: A real transformer does not change the frequency level of the electrical signal passing through it. This is because transformers operate on the principle of electromagnetic induction which does not affect the frequency of the signal. While transformers change voltage and current levels to match the energy requirements of different electrical systems, they maintain the same frequency as the input signal. Voltage and current levels are adjusted based on the turns ratio of the transformer, and real transformers experience minor power losses due to inefficiencies like heat and magnetic losses.

questions.text: Harmonics in transformer result in
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When harmonics are present in a transformer, they can cause an increase in core loss. Core loss is the power that is dissipated in the transformer's core due to hysteresis and eddy current losses. Harmonics cause the magnetic flux in the core to be distorted, which can increase the hysteresis and eddy current losses, leading to an increase in core loss.
The presence of harmonics distorts the current waveform, increasing resistive losses in the transformer's windings, known as I2R losses.
Harmonics can generate electromagnetic interference (EMI), adversely affecting communication equipment such as radios, televisions, and computers.
Harmonics in transformers can lead to increased core and I2R losses and cause electromagnetic interference, affecting communication circuits.

questions.explanation: All of the above effects result from the presence of harmonics in transformers. Harmonics cause distortion in the magnetic flux, leading to increased core losses due to heightened hysteresis and eddy currents. Additionally, distorted current waveforms increase I2R losses in the windings. Furthermore, harmonics can introduce electromagnetic interference (EMI), disrupting communication circuits. Therefore, the correct answer is All of the above. Each individual effect contributes to the overall impact of harmonics in transformers, making option D the most comprehensive choice.

questions.text: Power transformers have maximum efficiency at
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At no load, a transformer has minimal current flowing, but it still incurs core losses, making it inefficient.

The efficiency of the transformer can be obtained from a number of experiments conducted on different loads. The efficiency of a power transformer is always above 90%, thus it can be called a highly efficient device. They are operated on full load, hence are designed for maximum efficiency at full load.
While efficiency might be decent at half load, it is not optimized. Transformers are not specifically designed for maximum efficiency at half load.
Operating a transformer at double its rated load would lead to excessive losses and potential overheating, reducing efficiency and risking damage.

questions.explanation: Power transformers are designed to achieve maximum efficiency at full load because this is the condition under which they typically operate in real-world applications. At full load, the transformer balances core and copper losses, resulting in high efficiency, often above 90%. Operating at no load or half load does not optimize these losses, and double load conditions are beyond the transformer's rated capacity, leading to inefficiency and potential damage.

questions.text: When Ns>Np then transformer is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a transformer, the ratio of the number of turns in the secondary coil (Ns) to the number of turns in the primary coil (Np) determines whether the transformer is a step-up or step-down transformer. When Ns is greater than Np (Ns > Np), it means that the secondary coil has more turns than the primary coil. In a step-up transformer, the voltage is increased from the primary to the secondary coil. This means that the output voltage is higher than the input voltage. Since Ns > Np indicates that the secondary coil has more turns, it results in a higher voltage in the secondary coil compared to the primary coil.
A step-down transformer decreases the voltage of an AC signal. It does this by having fewer turns in the secondary coil than in the primary coil. The turns ratio is less than 1.
The primary coil is the input coil of the transformer. It is the coil that receives the input voltage.
The secondary coil is the output coil of the transformer. It is the coil that delivers the output voltage.

questions.explanation: When Ns>Np in a transformer, it is a step-up transformer.Here's what these variables represent:Ns is the number of turns in the secondary coil (the coil on the output side).Np is the number of turns in the primary coil (the coil on the input side).In a step-up transformer, the secondary coil has more turns than the primary coil. This configuration is designed to increase the voltage on the secondary side relative to the voltage on the primary side while reducing the current. Step-up transformers are commonly used in power transmission and distribution to increase voltage for long-distance transmission, which reduces energy losses due to lower current levels.

questions.text: Step down transformer has _ turns in secondary coil:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A step-down transformer has fewer turns in its secondary coil, not more. If it had more turns in the secondary coil, it would be a step-up transformer, which increases the voltage from the primary coil to the secondary coil.

A step-down transformer has fewer turns in its secondary coil compared to its primary coil. This design allows it to reduce the voltage from the primary coil to the secondary coil while increasing the current proportionally according to the transformer's turns ratio.

In a step-down transformer, the number of turns in the secondary coil is not equal to the number of turns in the primary coil. If they were equal, there would be no change in voltage, and it wouldn't serve the purpose of stepping down the voltage.

There are no practical transformers with zero turns in the secondary coil. Transformers work based on electromagnetic induction, where changing magnetic fields induce a voltage in the secondary coil. Zero turns would imply no coil, and thus no transformation or induction.

questions.explanation: In a step-down transformer, the number of turns in the secondary coil is less than the number of turns in the primary coil. This configuration is designed to decrease the input voltage to a lower output voltage.According to the transformer equation:Vp/Vs = Np/NsSince the step-down transformer is designed to decrease the voltage, the secondary voltage Vs is less than the primary voltage Vp. Therefore, to maintain the equation, the number of turns in the secondary coil (Ns) must be less than the number of turns in the primary coil (Np).

questions.text: For an efficient step down transformer:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Voltage in primary and secondary are equal
options.explanation: a) Voltage in primary and secondary are equal:Option (a) "Voltage in primary and secondary are equal" is not the correct answer. In a step-down transformer, the voltage in the secondary coil is lower than the voltage in the primary coil. This is achieved by having more turns in the primary coil, resulting in a higher voltage, and fewer turns in the secondary coil, leading to a lower voltage.

options.text: Current in primary and secondary are equal
options.explanation: b) Current in primary and secondary are equal:Option (b) "Current in primary and secondary are equal" is not the correct answer either. The current in the primary and secondary coils of a transformer is related to the turns ratio and the voltage ratio. Since the voltage is lower in the secondary coil of a step-down transformer, the current in the secondary coil will be higher than the current in the primary coil, and they are not equal.

options.text: Input power is same as the output power
options.explanation: c) Input power is same as the output power:Option (c) "Input power is the same as the output power" is the correct answer. In an ideal and efficient transformer, neglecting losses, the input power is equal to the output power. Power is defined as the product of voltage and current. In a transformer, the voltage is stepped down in the secondary coil while the current is stepped up. For an efficient transformer, the reduction in voltage is compensated by the increase in current, such that the input power is equal to the output power.

d) Output power is zero:Option (d) "Output power is zero" is not the correct answer. If the output power were zero, it would mean that the transformer is not transferring any energy to the load, which would make it ineffective.

questions.explanation: A step-up transformer is one that increases voltage, whereas a step-down transformer decreases voltage.Assuming, as we have, that resistance is negligible, the electrical power output of a transformer equals its input.

questions.text: A transformer is used to light 100 W 25 volt lamp from 250 Volt ac mains. The current in the main cable is 0.5 A. Calculate the efficiency of the transformer
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: this is not a correct option.
this is not a correct option.
this is not a correct option.
efficiency of a transformer can be calculated as:Efficiency= ouput/input x 100 = 100 x 100/ 250 x 0.5= 80%

questions.explanation: efficiency of a transformer can be calculated as:Efficiency= ouput/input x 100 = 100 x 100/ 250 x 0.5 = 80%

questions.text: The insulation between sheets of the transformer core is to get small.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The insulation between sheets of the transformer core is not intended to reduce the size of the hysteresis loop. The hysteresis loop refers to the phenomenon of energy loss due to the magnetization and demagnetization of the core material. Insulation between sheets of the transformer core helps prevent electrical short circuits and the flow of eddy currents, but it does not directly affect the size of the hysteresis loop.
Insulation material between laminations is required for electrical isolation so as to reduce Eddy current loss.

This option is incorrect. While insulation between sheets of the transformer core does help reduce eddy currents, it does not directly affect the size of the hysteresis loop. As mentioned earlier, the hysteresis loop refers to energy loss due to magnetic changes in the core material, and insulation does not directly impact this phenomenon.

This option is incorrect. Insulation between the sheets of the transformer core is indeed important in reducing eddy currents and preventing electrical short circuits. It is a necessary measure to ensure efficient and safe transformer operation. However, it does not directly affect the size of the hysteresis loop.

questions.explanation: Insulating material between laminations is required for electrical isolation so as to reduce eddy current loss.

questions.text: In step down transformer _ is decreased in secondary coils.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric field: While transformers do involve electric fields, the decrease in voltage is not directly related to a reduction in the electric field.

Magnetic field: The magnetic field is essential for the transformer's operation, but its strength doesn't directly dictate the voltage reduction. The changing magnetic field induces a voltage in both coils.
In step down transformer, Ns < N pStep down transformer reduces voltage and increases current.
None of these: This is incorrect as the number of turns is the key factor in a step-down transformer.

questions.explanation: l.In step down transformer, Ns < N pStep down transformer reduces voltage and increases current.

questions.text: The principle of transformer is?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A is correct as a Transformer works on the principle of “Mutual Inductance”.When two coils are brought in proximity with each other the magnetic field in one of the coils tend to link with the other. This further leads to the generation of voltage in the second coil. This property of a coil which affects or changes the current and voltage in a secondary coil is called mutual inductance.
Self-induction is not the principle of a transformer. Self-induction is the ability of a coil to induce an electromotive force in itself when the current changes.
Motional emf is unrelated to transformers. It refers to the electromotive force generated in a conductor moving in a magnetic field.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct as when a transformer's primary coil has an AC flowing through it; magnetic flux surrounds the conductor in proportion to the amount of current. The expanding and contracting flux cuts the secondary coil and induces a voltage in it. This is "mutual induction".

questions.text: In step up transformer?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A (Vs/Vp = 1) is incorrect because in a step-up transformer, the secondary voltage (Vs) is greater than the primary voltage (Vp).
Option B (Vs < Vp) is incorrect because, as mentioned earlier, Vs is greater than Vp in a step-up transformer.

Option C (Vs = Vp) is incorrect because, in an ideal transformer with no losses, the voltage ratio between the primary and secondary windings can never be equal. It will always be different unless the transformer is an autotransformer (which is a special case).
D is correct because a step transformer increases the input voltage. So Voltage in secondary should be greater than Voltage in Primary. Therefore,Vs>Vp.

questions.explanation: Step-up transformer is a transformer that increases the voltage from primary to secondary. Hence "Vs>Vp".

questions.text: The primary and secondary powers of a transformer are 200W and 100 W respectively, the efficiency of a transformer is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A is correct.Efficiency %= Output Power/Input Power x 100η % = 100/200 x 100η % = 0.5 x 100η % = 50%
Efficiency %= Output Power/Input Power x 100η % = 100/200 x 100η % = 0.5 x 100η % = 50%
Efficiency %= Output Power/Input Power x 100η % = 100/200 x 100η % = 0.5 x 100η % = 50%
Efficiency %= Output Power/Input Power x 100η % = 100/200 x 100η % = 0.5 x 100η % = 50%

questions.explanation: The formula for the efficiency of a transformer is given by, Efficiency η = Output power / input power X 100 = output power / output power + losses X 100 . Primary power is the input power, and secondary is the output power Efficiency η = 100W/200W X 100 Efficiency η = ½ x 100Efficiency η = "50%"

questions.text: The transformer laminations are insulated from each other by?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A (Mica strip): Mica strips are often used as insulation material in electrical machines due to their excellent thermal and electrical properties, but they are not typically used to insulate transformer laminations from each other. Instead, they are commonly used for slot liners or wedges in certain types of motors and generators.

Option B (Paper): While paper can be used as an insulation material in some electrical applications, it is not commonly used to insulate transformer laminations from each other. Paper is more commonly used for winding insulation or as an insulating material between windings and the core.
C is correct as we use a thin coating of varnish to insulate laminations of transformers. These insulation reduces the effect of Eddy Currents.
Option D (Any of the above): This option is incorrect because both Option A and Option B are not typical methods for insulating transformer laminations from each other, as explained above.

questions.explanation: These laminations are insulated from each other by a "thin coat of varnish" or paper to increase the effective resistivity of the core, thereby increasing the overall resistance to limit the flow of the eddy currents.

questions.text: Two long straight parallel wires held vertically have equal but opposite currents as shown in the figure. Which of the following effects will be observed?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Magnetic field at ‘X’ is stronger than that at ‘Y’ and ‘Z’
Magnetic field at ‘X’ is weaker than that at ‘Y’ and ‘Z’
Magnetic field at ‘X’, ‘Y’ and ‘Z’ is same
Magnetic field at ‘X’ is weaker than that at ‘Y’ but stronger than that at ‘Z’.
Magnetic field at ‘Y’ is stronger than that at ‘X’ and ‘Z’
options.explanation: When two parallel wires carry equal and opposite currents, they create a magnetic field around them. The direction of the magnetic field at any point is given by the right-hand rule. Now, let's consider the magnetic field at points X, Y, and Z. At point Y, the magnetic fields created by the two wires are in the same direction and add up, resulting in a stronger magnetic field than at either X or Z.At point X, the magnetic field due to the current in the left wire is in the upward direction, and the magnetic field due to the current in the right wire is in the downward direction. These two magnetic fields oppose each other, resulting in a weaker magnetic field than at point Y.At point Z, the magnetic field due to the current in the right wire is in the upward direction, and the magnetic field due to the current in the left wire is in the downward direction. These two magnetic fields also oppose each other, resulting in a weaker magnetic field than at point Y.Therefore, the magnetic field at Y is stronger than that at X and Z.

questions.explanation: When two parallel wires carry equal and opposite currents, they create a magnetic field around them. The direction of the magnetic field at any point is given by the right-hand rule. Now, let's consider the magnetic field at points X, Y, and Z. At point Y, the magnetic fields created by the two wires are in the same direction and add up, resulting in a stronger magnetic field than at either X or Z.At point X, the magnetic field due to the current in the left wire is in the upward direction, and the magnetic field due to the current in the right wire is in the downward direction. These two magnetic fields oppose each other, resulting in a weaker magnetic field than at point Y.At point Z, the magnetic field due to the current in the right wire is in the upward direction, and the magnetic field due to the current in the left wire is in the downward direction. These two magnetic fields also oppose each other, resulting in a weaker magnetic field than at point Y.Therefore, the magnetic field at Y is stronger than that at X and Z.

questions.text: Magnetic field is set up in a region around a conductor, carrying:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When charges are in motion, they have current (AC, DC or pulsating DC); magnetic field is produced around a current carrying conductor due to the flow of these charges.
When charges are in motion, they have current (AC, DC or pulsating DC); magnetic field is produced around a current carrying conductor due to the flow of these charge.
When charges are in motion, they have current (AC, DC or pulsating DC); magnetic field is produced around a current carrying conductor due to the flow of these charge.
As all the three options are correct so this option is correct.

questions.explanation: When charges are in motion, they have current (AC, DC or pulsating DC); magnetic field is produced around a current carrying conductor due to the flow of these charge.andMagnetic field is created whenever an electric current flows through a conductor. This is because a moving electrical charge creates a magnetic field. The strength and direction of the magnetic field depend on the amount of current flowing and the direction of flow. Magnetic fields can also be created by permanent magnets (bar magnets). These are made from materials that are naturally good conductors of electricity, such as iron. The molecules in these materials line up in rows, which creates a strong magnetic field. When you bring a conductor close to a magnet, the magnetic field from the magnet affects the moving charges in the conductor. This causes current flow, creating a current-carrying conductor, which creates its own magnetic field. These two fields interact with each other, resulting in a force that either attracts or repels the objects.

questions.text: A transformer is said to be 100 % efficient if:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Efficiency is not determined by the equality of output and input voltage. In fact, transformers are designed to change the voltage while maintaining power transfer efficiency. So, having different output and input voltages is normal, and a transformer can still be 100% efficient even if the voltages are different.

This option is correct. For an ideal transformer, Power input = Power output. As the efficiency of a transformer is given by E= Output Power/ Input Power x 100, the efficiency of an ideal transformer will be 100 percent.

This is also incorrect. Efficiency is not based on the equality of output and input current. In a transformer, the output current and input current can be different due to the voltage transformation, and a transformer can still be 100% efficient if it has no losses.
This option is incorrect. As the efficiency of a transformer is given by E= Output Power/ Input Power x 100.

questions.explanation: For an ideal transformer (100% efficiency) Power input = Power output

questions.text: If a wire having current 10A has a 3T magnetic field, what will be the magnetic field at a point from the wire at double the distance?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is: B = µ0I/2πr It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.

questions.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is B = µ0I/2πr. It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will reduce by a factor of two, which is 1.5 T.

questions.text: Two long parallel wires Wire 1 and Wire 2 repel each other as shown in the figure. What could be the reasons?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Both carry current in same direction
options.explanation: They will attract each other in this case.

options.text: Both carry current in opposite direction
options.explanation: Two parallel wires have currents travelling in opposite directions, the magnetic fields generated by those currents between the wires will both point in the same direction (use right hand grip rule). Thus, wires would repel each other.

options.text: Wire 1 has current, but Wire 2 has no current
options.explanation: They repel each other because both carry current in opposite directions.

options.text: Wire 2 has current, Wire 1 has no current

questions.text: In the actual transformer, the output is always:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. In real-world transformers, there are always losses, such as resistive losses in the windings, core losses, and sometimes leakage inductance. These losses result in some of the input power being dissipated as heat, so the output power is less than the input power.

This option is also incorrect. A transformer is a passive device, which means it cannot create energy. It canonly transform energy from one voltage level to another. Therefore, the output power of a transformer can never be more than the input power. This violates the law of conservation of energy.

This option is correct. In actual transformers, the output power is typically less than the input power due tothe losses mentioned earlier. These losses result in reduced efficiency, and the output power is a fraction of the input power.

This option is incorrect. The concept of the output power of a transformer being infinity is not physicallypossible. Transformers have limits based on their design and the materials used. It's important to note that no real-world device can have an output power equal to or greater than infinity.

questions.explanation: For an ideal transformer: Power Input = Power Output But in case of actual transformer Power Input > Power Output.

questions.text: The stator and the rotor of an A.C. motor are connected by a:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as there is no physical connection between them.
This option is incorrect as there is no physical connection between them.
This option is incorrect as there is no physical connection between them.

In an AC motor, the stator and rotor are not physically connected. Instead, their interaction occurs through the electromagnetic fields generated by the stator windings and the rotor windings. The stator creates a rotating magnetic field due to the alternating current passing through its coils, which induces a magnetic field in the rotor, causing it to rotate. There's no direct physical connection between the stator and rotor in most AC motor designs.

questions.explanation: In an AC motor, the stator and rotor are not physically connected. Instead, their interaction occurs through the electromagnetic fields generated by the stator windings and the rotor windings. The stator creates a rotating magnetic field due to the alternating current passing through its coils, which induces a magnetic field in the rotor, causing it to rotate. There's no direct physical connection between the stator and rotor in most AC motor designs.

questions.text: A coil having number of turns N and cross-sectional area A is rotated in a uniform magnetic field B with an angular velocity ω. The maximum value of the emf induced in it is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that the induced emf is inversely proportional to the angular velocity, which is incorrect. The induced emf should increase with angular velocity.
This is the correct formula. The induced emf is directly proportional to the number of turns, the magnetic field, the area of the coil, and the angular velocity.
This option implies that the induced emf decreases with the square of the angular velocity, which is incorrect. There is no square in the relationship.
This option incorrectly suggests that the induced emf is proportional to the square of the angular velocity, which is not supported by the formula for induced emf.

questions.explanation: The flux linking with the coil at any instant t is given as:Φ = NBA cos ωt ∴ E= -dΦ/dt = NBAω sinωt Therefore, the maximum value of emf is Emax = NBAω Hence, Option B is correct.
